title: Chudnovsky binary-splitting reference impl: (P,Q,T) per term-range + single-host final assembly to >= 1e6 digits
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. This is the Method A reference implementation - the single-host baseline that every later distributed/merged result must reproduce bit-identically, so its determinism and reference-match are foundational. It depends on the arch-001 ADRs (backend choice and guard-digit policy).
objective: Implement the Chudnovsky binary-splitting algorithm computing exact integer (P,Q,T) per term-range plus single-host final assembly to >= 1e6 correct digits of pi.
acceptanceCriteria: Computes exact integer (P, Q, T) for any term-range [a, b) using the associative recurrence; leaf terms follow the Chudnovsky definition; N ~= digits / 14.18 documented.
Single-host final assembly pi ~= (426880*sqrt(10005)*Q)/T produces >= 1e6 correct digits.
Result matches a published reference prefix to the computed precision (minus declared guard digits).
Deterministic: identical input produces identical artifact hash on repeat runs.
TypeScript/ESM, builds via pnpm; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; MIT-licensed.
output: A PR adding the Chudnovsky binary-splitting reference implementation (per-term-range (P,Q,T) plus single-host final assembly) computing pi to >= 1e6 verified digits.
